operating_state: Total revenue declined to $28.3 billion from $36.6 billion driven by lower commodity prices with WTI crude averaging $77.64 versus $94.23 in 2022. Oil and gas production increased 6% to 1,367 Mboe/d with Permian Basin operations contributing significantly, while chemical operations generated third-highest annual earnings despite weakened global demand. Operations face increasing federal regulatory constraints with BOEM conducting only three potential GOM lease sales for 2024-2029 and BLM resuming limited onshore leasing.
cost_structure: Capital expenditures totaled $5.5 billion primarily allocated to oil and gas development, while operating expenses increased driven by higher workover and maintenance activity. The IRA imposed 15% corporate alternative minimum tax on adjusted financial statement income exceeding $1 billion and methane emissions charges starting at $900 per metric ton in 2024, rising to $1,500 by 2026. Enhanced environmental compliance costs and service provider cost inflation from equipment shortages elevated operational expenses.
material_events: Completed $12 billion CrownRock acquisition funded through $9.1 billion new debt, $1.7 billion equity issuance, and assumption of existing debt, with closing expected by December 2024 subject to FTC regulatory review. Completed acquisition of remaining 68% interest in Carbon Engineering, recognizing $283 million fair value gain. Redeemed $1.5 billion face value preferred stock and repurchased $1.8 billion common shares. Credit rating upgraded to investment grade by Moody's and Fitch.
macro_exposures: Operations highly dependent on oil price volatility driven by geopolitical risks including Russia-Ukraine war and Middle East conflicts, OPEC supply actions, and U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve management. Domestic PVC demand declined 13% year-over-year with high interest rates and inflation constraining housing and construction sectors. Federal agencies implementing expanded NEPA reviews requiring climate change impact assessments using higher social cost of GHG estimates in permitting decisions.
forward_outlook: Management committed to completing strategic divestitures within 18 months following CrownRock acquisition closure and repaying at least $4.5 billion debt within 12 months using divestiture proceeds and excess cash flows. STRATOS direct air capture facility expected commercially operational mid-2025. Quarterly dividend increased to $0.22 per share beginning February 2024, with shareholder return priorities focusing on growing sustainable dividend and reducing debt below $15 billion before resuming share repurchases.
uncertainty_profile: CrownRock acquisition subject to FTC regulatory review with extended waiting period under HSR Act and potential extensions to December 2025 if antitrust proceedings arise. Integration risks include potential unknown liabilities not covered by limited representation and warranty insurance. Faces potential $2.0 billion tax liability repayment related to Anadarko's disputed Tronox settlement with Tax Court proceedings ongoing. Environmental remediation liabilities could exceed recorded amounts while net-zero emissions goals require significant future costs dependent on securing project financing.
